learningObjectives: Use core health and lifestyle vocabulary at A2 level.
Use should, must and need to to give and understand health advice.
Use a few and a little correctly with countable and uncountable nouns.
Understand vocabulary in context in a short health article.
Understand attitude and opinion in a health podcast recording.
Write a short note about symptoms and an appointment request.
Answer interview questions about health habits and lifestyle.
grammarFocus: Countable / uncountable with a few / a little

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I have a doctor's appointment at ten.
A cough is a common symptom of a cold.
She recovered after a week of rest.
The doctor gave me a prescription for medicine.
He has an allergy to milk.
She got a leg injury playing football.
The treatment helped him feel better.
Hand washing is good prevention against illness.
The doctor checked my symptoms carefully.
A little exercise every day keeps you healthy.
Eat healthy food and sleep well.

grammarReference.explanation: Use should for advice (You should rest), must for strong rules or necessity (You must wear a mask), and need to for something necessary (You need to take medicine). After modals, use the base verb without to (except need to).
examples: You should drink warm water when you have a cough.
You must not share cups when you are ill.
You need to finish all your medicine.
Patients should make an appointment before visiting.
commonMistakes: You should to rest (×) → You should rest (✓)
You must wearing a mask (×) → You must wear a mask (✓)
You need finish the treatment (×) → You need to finish the treatment (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Countable / uncountable with a few / a little
grammarReference.explanation: Use a few + countable plural nouns (a few nuts, a few symptoms). Use a little + uncountable nouns (a little water, a little exercise). Some/any follow the same countable/uncountable rules.
examples: She has a few symptoms: cough and fever.
Drink a little warm water before bed.
A few minutes of exercise help prevention.
There is a little medicine left in the bottle.
commonMistakes: A little symptoms (×) → A few symptoms (✓)
A few water (×) → A little water (✓)
Few exercise every day (×) → A little exercise every day (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Health and Lifestyle Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve health and lifestyle words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Health Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A planned meeting with a doctor is an _____.
questions.explanation: Appointment (cuộc hẹn) là lịch gặp bác sĩ. Treatment là điều trị.

questions.questionText: A sign that you are ill, like a cough or fever, is a _____.
questions.explanation: Symptom (triệu chứng) báo hiệu bệnh. Prevention là phòng ngừa.

questions.questionText: To get healthy again after an illness means to _____.
questions.explanation: Recover (hồi phục) nghĩa là khỏe lại. Allergy là dị ứng.

questions.questionText: A piece of paper from a doctor for medicine is a _____.
questions.explanation: Prescription (đơn thuốc) do bác sĩ kê. Symptom là triệu chứng.

questions.questionText: A bad reaction to food like nuts or milk is an _____.
questions.explanation: Allergy (dị ứng) phản ứng với thực phẩm/chất. Injury là chấn thương.

questions.questionText: Damage to your body from an accident is an _____.
questions.explanation: Injury (chấn thương) do tai nạn. Symptom là biểu hiện bệnh.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the health words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với nghĩa rõ ràng. Treatment và prevention thường bị nhầm.

pairs.right: medical care to make you better
actions to stop illness before it starts
a person who diagnoses and treats patients

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: The _____ gave Lan a prescription after checking her cough.
questions.explanation: Doctor (bác sĩ) khám và kê đơn. Nurse thường hỗ trợ điều trị.

questions.questionText: Dr Nguyen says _____ is better than waiting for treatment.
questions.explanation: Prevention is better than treatment — phòng bệnh hơn chữa bệnh.

questions.questionText: After the football match, he had a knee _____ and went to hospital.
questions.explanation: Injury (chấn thương) sau tai nạn thể thao. Allergy là dị ứng.

questions.questionText: Eat _____ food and do a little _____ every day.
questions.explanation: Healthy food + exercise — thói quen sống khỏe.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Health Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word.

questions.questionText: Complete: I made an [0] with the doctor. | My main [1] is a sore throat. | The [2] gave me medicine. | I will [3] in a few days.
questions.explanation: appointment; symptom; nurse/doctor — prescription context; recover.

content.template: I made an [0] with the doctor. My main [1] is a sore throat. The nurse gave me [2] from the doctor. I will [3] in a few days.

exercises.title: Review: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: The _____ gave Lan a prescription after checking her cough.
questions.explanation: Doctor (bác sĩ) khám và kê đơn. Nurse thường hỗ trợ điều trị.

questions.questionText: Dr Nguyen says _____ is better than waiting for treatment.
questions.explanation: Prevention is better than treatment — phòng bệnh hơn chữa bệnh.

questions.questionText: After the football match, he had a knee _____ and went to hospital.
questions.explanation: Injury (chấn thương) sau tai nạn thể thao. Allergy là dị ứng.

questions.questionText: Eat _____ food and do a little _____ every day.
questions.explanation: Healthy food + exercise — thói quen sống khỏe.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Health Advice and Quantifiers
lessons.learningObjective: Use should, must and need to for health advice, and a few / a little with nouns.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Correct Form
exercises.instructions: Choose the best word or phrase for each sentence.

questions.questionText: You _____ rest when you have a fever.
questions.explanation: Should = lời khuyên: You should rest.

questions.questionText: You _____ wear a mask in the clinic. It is a rule.
questions.explanation: Must = quy định bắt buộc.

questions.questionText: Drink _____ warm water before bed.
questions.explanation: Water không đếm được → a little.

questions.questionText: She has _____ symptoms: cough and headache.
questions.explanation: Symptoms đếm được → a few.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: You [0] see a doctor if symptoms continue. | You [1] to finish your medicine. | Drink [2] water.
questions.explanation: should (lời khuyên); need (need to); a little (nước không đếm được).

content.template: You [0] see a doctor if symptoms continue. You [1] to finish your medicine. Drink [2] water.

questions.questionText: Complete: You [0] not share cups when you are ill. | I have [1] apples for a healthy snack. | Do [2] exercise every day.
questions.explanation: must (cấm); a few (apples đếm được); a little (exercise không đếm).

content.template: You [0] not share cups when you are ill. I have [1] apples for a healthy snack. Do [2] exercise every day.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Patients _____ make an appointment before visiting the clinic.
questions.explanation: Should = khuyên nên đặt lịch trước.

questions.questionText: There is _____ medicine left. Take it tonight.
questions.explanation: Medicine không đếm được → a little.

questions.questionText: You _____ tell the doctor about your allergy to nuts.
questions.explanation: Must = bắt buộc thông tin quan trọng.

questions.questionText: He felt better after _____ days of treatment.
questions.explanation: Days đếm được → a few days.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: You / should / drink / a little / warm water.
questions.explanation: You should drink a little warm water. — modal + a little + uncountable.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: You / must / not / share / cups.
questions.explanation: You must not share cups. — must not + base verb.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / have / a few / symptoms / today.
questions.explanation: I have a few symptoms today. — a few + countable plural.

exercises.title: Review: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Patients _____ make an appointment before visiting the clinic.
questions.explanation: Should = khuyên nên đặt lịch trước.

questions.questionText: There is _____ medicine left. Take it tonight.
questions.explanation: Medicine không đếm được → a little.

questions.questionText: You _____ tell the doctor about your allergy to nuts.
questions.explanation: Must = bắt buộc thông tin quan trọng.

questions.questionText: He felt better after _____ days of treatment.
questions.explanation: Days đếm được → a few days.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Health Article
lessons.learningObjective: Understand health vocabulary in context and identify advice in a short article.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Health Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Staying Healthy This Winter
passage.text:
HEALTH TIPS FROM DR NGUYEN

Winter is a busy time at our clinic. Many patients call to make an appointment because of cold symptoms such as a cough or fever. Dr Nguyen says prevention is better than treatment.

"Wash your hands often and eat healthy food," she advises. "If you have an allergy to nuts, check food labels carefully. A few nuts can cause a serious problem."

When you feel unwell, note your symptoms and rest at home. You should drink a little warm water and sleep well. You must not share cups with family members if you are ill.

For a bad injury, go to hospital immediately. The nurse will clean the wound and give you a prescription for medicine. Most people recover in a week if they follow the doctor's advice.

Remember: you need to finish all your medicine, even when you feel better. If symptoms continue, book another appointment. Prevention, rest and good habits help you stay healthy all year.
passage.imagePrompt: A friendly doctor in a clinic giving health advice on a poster about symptoms, prevention and appointments; clean medical setting.

questions.questionText: What should people with a nut allergy do?
questions.explanation: Check food labels carefully if you have an allergy to nuts.

questions.questionText: What must ill people not do with family cups?
questions.explanation: You must not share cups with family members if you are ill.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Staying Healthy This Winter
passage.text:
HEALTH TIPS FROM DR NGUYEN

Winter is a busy time at our clinic. Many patients call to make an appointment because of cold symptoms such as a cough or fever. Dr Nguyen says prevention is better than treatment.

"Wash your hands often and eat healthy food," she advises. "If you have an allergy to nuts, check food labels carefully. A few nuts can cause a serious problem."

When you feel unwell, note your symptoms and rest at home. You should drink a little warm water and sleep well. You must not share cups with family members if you are ill.

For a bad injury, go to hospital immediately. The nurse will clean the wound and give you a prescription for medicine. Most people recover in a week if they follow the doctor's advice.

Remember: you need to finish all your medicine, even when you feel better. If symptoms continue, book another appointment. Prevention, rest and good habits help you stay healthy all year.
passage.imagePrompt: A friendly doctor in a clinic giving health advice on a poster about symptoms, prevention and appointments; clean medical setting.

questions.questionText: In the article, 'prevention' means _____.
questions.explanation: Prevention is better than treatment — ngăn bệnh trước khi cần điều trị.

questions.questionText: Why does Dr Nguyen mention finishing all medicine?
questions.explanation: Even when you feel better — để điều trị hiệu quả, tránh tái phát.

choices.text: To make sure treatment works completely

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the article one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: Staying Healthy This Winter
passage.text:
HEALTH TIPS FROM DR NGUYEN

Winter is a busy time at our clinic. Many patients call to make an appointment because of cold symptoms such as a cough or fever. Dr Nguyen says prevention is better than treatment.

"Wash your hands often and eat healthy food," she advises. "If you have an allergy to nuts, check food labels carefully. A few nuts can cause a serious problem."

When you feel unwell, note your symptoms and rest at home. You should drink a little warm water and sleep well. You must not share cups with family members if you are ill.

For a bad injury, go to hospital immediately. The nurse will clean the wound and give you a prescription for medicine. Most people recover in a week if they follow the doctor's advice.

Remember: you need to finish all your medicine, even when you feel better. If symptoms continue, book another appointment. Prevention, rest and good habits help you stay healthy all year.
passage.imagePrompt: A friendly doctor in a clinic giving health advice on a poster about symptoms, prevention and appointments; clean medical setting.

questions.questionText: What is the main message of the article?
questions.explanation: Bài nhấn mạnh prevention, rest và thói quen tốt để khỏe cả năm.

choices.text: Good habits and prevention help you stay healthy
You should never see a doctor

questions.questionText: Put the advice in order: (1) Rest and note symptoms (2) Prevention habits (3) Finish medicine and follow-up
questions.explanation: Bài: prevention trước → khi ốm nghỉ/ngờ symptom → hoàn thành thuốc.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Article
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

questions.questionText: Match each topic to a fact from the health article.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với lời khuyên cụ thể của Dr Nguyen.

pairs.right: About a week with doctor's advice
Drink a little warm water and rest

exercises.title: Review: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the article one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: At the Clinic
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to clinic conversations and a health podcast to understand advice and opinions.

exercises.title: Learn: Doctor's Appointment
exercises.instructions: Listen to the conversation. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: At the Doctor's Surgery

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Good morning. Do you have an appointment today?

lines.speaker: Patient
lines.text: Yes. My name is Lan. I booked online yesterday.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: Please sit down. The doctor is busy with another patient.

lines.speaker: Patient
lines.text: Thank you. I have had a bad cough for three days.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: That is a common symptom in winter. Have you taken any medicine?

lines.speaker: Patient
lines.text: No. I thought I should rest first. I also have a little fever.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: The doctor will check you soon. You must wear a mask in the clinic.

script.audioNotes: Clear clinic dialogue, calm tone. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: Does the patient have an appointment?
questions.explanation: Yes, she booked online yesterday.

questions.questionText: What symptom does the patient mention first?
questions.explanation: I have had a bad cough for three days.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Clinic
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same conversation. Answer the next questions.

script.title: At the Doctor's Surgery

lines.speaker: Receptionist

lines.speaker: Patient

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: The doctor will check you soon. You must wear a mask in the clinic.

script.audioNotes: Clear clinic dialogue, calm tone. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: What other symptom does the patient have?
questions.explanation: I also have a little fever.

questions.questionText: What must patients do in the clinic?
questions.explanation: You must wear a mask in the clinic.

exercises.title: Check: Health Podcast
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new recording. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Health Podcast — Exercise and Prevention

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Welcome to Healthy Living. Today we talk about exercise and prevention.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: You should do a little exercise every day, even a short walk. A few minutes can help.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: For injury prevention, warm up before sport. You must wear good shoes when you run.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: If you have an allergy, tell your coach. Some people need to avoid certain foods.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Remember, treatment is important, but healthy habits help you recover faster too.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Next week we will discuss sleep and stress. Thanks for listening.

script.audioNotes: Friendly radio presenter, moderate pace. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What is the podcast mainly about?
questions.explanation: Today we talk about exercise and prevention.

questions.questionText: What does the host say about sport?
questions.explanation: Warm up before sport. You must wear good shoes when you run.

choices.text: Warm up and wear good shoes

questions.questionText: What is the host's opinion about treatment and habits?
questions.explanation: Treatment is important, but healthy habits help you recover faster too.

choices.text: Healthy habits help recovery as well as treatment

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Note About Symptoms
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short note about symptoms and request an appointment using health vocabulary.

exercises.title: Learn: Health Note Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: appointment, symptom, doctor, should, thank.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear [0], | I would like to make an [1]. | My main [2] is a sore throat. | I think I [3] rest at home.
questions.explanation: Doctor; appointment; symptom; should — khung ghi chú ngắn gửi phòng khám.

content.template:
Dear [0],

I would like to make an [1]. My main [2] is a sore throat. I think I [3] rest at home.

Thank you.

exercises.title: Practice: Build Health Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: You / should / drink / a little / water.
questions.explanation: You should drink a little water. — lời khuyên sức khỏe.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / need to / see / the doctor / tomorrow.
questions.explanation: I need to see the doctor tomorrow. — need to + động từ.

exercises.title: Check: Symptoms and Appointment Note
exercises.instructions: Write a short note to your doctor. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: You are not feeling well. Write a short note to Dr Nguyen to request an appointment and describe your symptoms.
prompts: Say you want to make an appointment.
Say what you have done so far (e.g. rest, drink water).
Ask when you can visit the clinic.
successCriteria: Uses should or need to appropriately

modelAnswer.text:
Dear Dr Nguyen,

I would like to make an appointment, please. I have had a cough and a little fever for three days. I have rested at home and drunk warm water, but I do not feel better. I think I should see you soon. When can I come to the clinic?

Thank you,
Lan

grammar.criteria: Uses should, must or need to correctly; a few/a little where appropriate.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses health words (appointment, symptom, recover, treatment) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Note has greeting, clear body and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Requests appointment with symptoms; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Note Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the note frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Nurse, | I have an [0] to nuts. | I had a small [1] yesterday. | The [2] helped me. | I hope to [3] soon.
questions.explanation: allergy; injury; treatment; recover — tóm tắt tình huống sức khỏe.

content.template:
Dear Nurse,

I have an [0] to nuts. I had a small [1] yesterday. The [2] helped me. I hope to [3] soon.

Thank you,
Minh

exercises.title: Review: Symptoms and Appointment Note
exercises.instructions: Write a short note to your doctor. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: You are not feeling well. Write a short note to Dr Nguyen to request an appointment and describe your symptoms.
prompts: Say you want to make an appointment.
Say what you have done so far (e.g. rest, drink water).
Ask when you can visit the clinic.
successCriteria: Uses should or need to appropriately

modelAnswer.text:
Dear Dr Nguyen,

I would like to make an appointment, please. I have had a cough and a little fever for three days. I have rested at home and drunk warm water, but I do not feel better. I think I should see you soon. When can I come to the clinic?

Thank you,
Lan

grammar.criteria: Uses should, must or need to correctly; a few/a little where appropriate.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses health words (appointment, symptom, recover, treatment) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Note has greeting, clear body and polite closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Requests appointment with symptoms; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Health
lessons.learningObjective: Answer questions about health habits, symptoms and lifestyle choices.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How do you stay healthy? You say:
questions.explanation: Healthy habits ngắn gọn: eat healthy food, exercise.

choices.text: I eat symptom food every day.
I eat healthy food and do a little exercise.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What should you do when you have a cough? You say:
questions.explanation: Should + lời khuyên: rest and drink water.

choices.text: I should to rest and drink.
I should rest and drink a little warm water.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Have you ever been to a doctor's appointment? You say:
questions.explanation: Trả lời có/không + chi tiết ngắn về appointment.

choices.text: Yes, I went last month because I had a fever.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Do you have any allergies? You say:
questions.explanation: Have an allergy to + thực phẩm — cấu trúc A2 chuẩn.

choices.text: Yes, I have injury to milk.
Yes, I have an allergy to milk.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What must you do in a hospital waiting room? You say:
questions.explanation: Must + quy tắc: be quiet, wear a mask.

choices.text: You must be quiet and wear a mask.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How long did it take you to recover from your cold? You say:
questions.explanation: Recover + thời gian: about a week.

choices.text: It took about a week to prescription.
It took about a week to recover.
It took about a week to allergy.

exercises.title: Check: Health and Lifestyle Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about health. Speak for up to two minutes.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Responses
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Your friend has a bad headache. You advise them:
questions.explanation: Should + lời khuyên thực tế: rest, see doctor if needed.

choices.text: You should rest. If it continues, make an appointment.
You must share cups with everyone.

questions.questionText: A nurse asks about your symptoms. You say:
questions.explanation: Liệt kê symptom rõ ràng.

choices.text: I have a prescription and injury.
I have a cough and a little fever.

questions.questionText: The examiner asks: Is prevention important? You say:
questions.explanation: Đưa ra ý kiến + lý do ngắn về prevention.

choices.text: Yes, prevention is important because it stops illness before treatment.

exercises.title: Review: Health and Lifestyle Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about health. Speak for up to two minutes.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.
